q: They walked (1) / besides (2) / each other (3) / in silence. (4)
His relation (1) / to Kalpana (2) / is (3) / good. (4)
The teacher (1) / has not (2) / control upon (3) / the students. (4)
We (1) / travelled (2) / with steamer (3) / to reach there. (4)
His statement (1) / is very much (2) / similar about (3) / yours. (4)
The shopkeeper (1) / deals with grain (2) / but did not deal (3) / honestly with me. (4)
correction: deals with grain → deals in grain

q: He (1) / appealed to the judge (2) / for his release (3) / from jail. (4)
I complained against (1) / the magistrate (2) / against Munna (3) / about his misconduct. (4)
We (1) / are (2) / responsible for God (3) / for our actions. (4)
The doctor referred (1) / the patient for the P.M.C.H. (2) / without (3) / examining him. (4)
correction: for the P.M.C.H. → to the P.M.C.H.

q: My cousin has been (1) / married with (2) / the richest man (3) / of the village. (4)
He was (1) / shocked to hear that (2) / her mother (3) / died of an accident. (4)
correction: died of an accident → died in an accident

q: Mr. Kisori Takur (1) / is (2) / blind (3) / with one eye. (4)
correction: with one eye → in one eye

q: On a holiday (1) / I prefer (2) / writing than going out (3) / visiting friends. (4)
correction: than going out → to going out

q: I have been working on (1) / the problem from a long time (2) / but is still not (3) / able to solve it. (4)
options: the problem from a long time
correction: from a long time → for a long time

q: Mr. Mishra and his wife (1) / were invited (2) / for the cultural function (3) / at my house. (4)
correction: for the cultural function → to the cultural function

q: Beside chocolate (1) / they also bought (2) / many balloons (3) / for the child. (4)
Yesterday, Binay (1) / came across with (2) / one of his (3) / old friends. (4)
Of what I know (1) / of her (2) / I hesitate to (3) / trust in him. (4)
correction: Of what I know → From what I know

q: He left to Delhi (1) / this morning (2) / for some (3) / official work. (4)
correction: left to Delhi → left for Delhi

q: He cannot (1) / prevent (2) / me (3) / to go there. (4)
My book has been (1) / missing (2) / from my room (3) / till yesterday. (4)
Akbar (1) / came on the throne (2) / at a very (3) / early age. (4)
correction: came on the throne → came to the throne

q: You (1) / must provide (2) / to your children (3) / against the evil day. (4)
correction: to your children → for your children

q: I will (1) / send you (2) / this book (3) / latest by 10th February. (4)
They all met in a room (1) / which was close to the centre (2) / to discuss about the plan of action. (3)
options: They all met in a room
which was close to the centre
to discuss about the plan of action.

q: When the police accused him with theft, (1) / nobody believed them, (2) / as he was well known for his honesty. (3)
options: When the police accused him with theft,
as he was well known for his honesty.
correction: accused him with → accused him of

q: The meeting scheduled to be held (1) / between 2 P.M. to 3 P.M. (2) / was postponed suddenly. (3)
options: The meeting scheduled to be held
correction: between 2 P.M. to 3 P.M. → from 2 P.M. to 3 P.M.

q: He was conferred with an honorary D. Litt. degree (1) / in recognition of his services (2) / to the cause of learning. (3)
options: He was conferred with an honorary D. Litt. degree

q: The myth that some races (1) / are naturally superior than others (2) / has now been fully demolished. (3)
This particular issue (1) / has been hanging in fire (2) / for a long time. (3)
correction: hanging in fire → hanging in the fire

q: In the past (1) / people travelled (2) / by foot. (3)
You should know (1) / that the ultimate decision in the matters (2) / rests upon the board of directors. (3)
options: that the ultimate decision in the matters
rests upon the board of directors.

q: In some tribal areas (1) / people worship snakes (2) / and are not scared from them. (3)
options: and are not scared from them.

q: His new proposal (1) / seems hardly different (2) / than that the one I've just rejected. (3)
options: than that the one I've just rejected.

q: To lead a well-balanced life (1) / you shall have to cultivate other interests (2) / beside your professional ones. (3)
options: you shall have to cultivate other interests
correction: beside your professional ones → besides your professional ones

q: After nagging constantly for months (1) / and administering weekly tests (2) / the teacher was at least pleased for the boys' progress. (3)
options: the teacher was at least pleased for the boys' progress.

q: I'd appreciate spending half an hour with you (1) / to discuss about how these and other experience (2) / might be mutually beneficial. (3)
options: I'd appreciate spending half an hour with you
to discuss about how these and other experience

q: The teacher was happy (1) / when he entered the class (2) / which comprised of a hundred students. (3)
options: which comprised of a hundred students.

q: Yet, firms and other concerns are spending large sums of money all the time (1) / and somehow, we take this for granted (2) / and never stop to wonder where it comes from. (3)
options: Yet, firms and other concerns are spending large sums of money all the time
and somehow, we take this for granted
and never stop to wonder where it comes from.

q: Thank for your invitation for lunch (1) / which I am very pleased (2) / to accept. (3)
options: Thank for your invitation for lunch

q: Both the boys (1) / came late in the hall (2) / and sat besides me. (3)
correction: in the hall → to the hall

q: I signed on the receipt (1) / in the morning but the pay (2) / has not been disbursed to me. (3)
options: in the morning but the pay
has not been disbursed to me.
correction: signed on the receipt → signed the receipt

q: In my opinion (1) / a pencil is always (2) / preferable than a pen. (3)
You must make up the (1) / time you wasted this afternoon (2) / by working late over to night. (3)
options: by working late over to night.
correction: working late over to night → working late tonight

q: The extraordinary ability of the satellites to (1) / see, listen and communicate has made them integral with (2) / modern warfare and nuclear war strategies in particular. (3)
options: The extraordinary ability of the satellites to
see, listen and communicate has made them integral with
modern warfare and nuclear war strategies in particular.

q: He looked like a lion (1) / baulked from (2) / its prey. (3)
Often in political campaigns, a point is reached at which (1) / the candidates take out their gloves (2) / and start slagging with bare fists. (3)
options: Often in political campaigns, a point is reached at which
the candidates take out their gloves
and start slagging with bare fists.

q: One of the major aims of the Air Force (1) / was the complete demolition of all means of transportation (2) / by the bombing of rail lines and terminals. (3)
options: One of the major aims of the Air Force
was the complete demolition of all means of transportation
by the bombing of rail lines and terminals.

q: The celebrated singer was (1) / surrounded by the usual crowd (2) / of lackeys and hangers-in. (3)
The aircraft was overloaded (1) / there was something wrong of the battery (2) / and the engine was making a queer noise. (3)
options: there was something wrong of the battery
and the engine was making a queer noise.
correction: wrong of the battery → wrong with the battery

q: I warned him (1) / for the danger (2) / he was going (3) / to face during the hiking. (4)
correction: for the danger → about the danger

q: Ordinarily, when in difficulty (1) / Renuka prefers keeping her counsel (2) / than running about (3) / taking advice. (4)
correction: than running about → rather than running about

q: Sheeila advised to (1) / her child not to (2) / play with the ball (3) / on the road. (4)
Basu was trying to pass (1) / through the gap on (2) / the fence when he (3) / tore his shirt. (4)
correction: through the gap on → through the gap in

q: I have been teaching in this school (1) / since several years (2) / but have never met (3) / such a hopeless class as this. (4)
options: I have been teaching in this school
such a hopeless class as this.
correction: since several years → for several years

q: It is easy to see that (1) / a lawyer's demeanour in court (2) / may be prejudicial against the interests of his client. (3)
options: It is easy to see that
may be prejudicial against the interests of his client.

q: The reason we have not been able to pay income tax (1) / is due to fact (2) / that we did not receive pay on time. (3)
options: The reason we have not been able to pay income tax
that we did not receive pay on time.
correction: due to fact → due to the fact

q: The policemen fired all the (1) / students when (2) / they were attacked (3) / by some of them. (4)
correction: fired all the students → fired at all the students

q: He was not promoted (1) / to the rank of colonel (2) / till for a few (3) / months of his resignation. (4)
correction: till for a few → until a few

q: All the players (1) / except Sam (2) / were present (3) / into the field. (4)
correction: into the field → on the field

q: Despite of a good monsoon (1) / this year, the production (2) / of food grains in the (3) / country did not go up. (4)
correction: Despite of a good monsoon → Despite a good monsoon

q: Yesterday, while (1) / crossing the road (2) / he was (3) / run out by a truck. (4)
correction: run out by → run over by

q: The girl wrote an essay (1) / so well (2) / that her teacher was exceedingly (3) / pleased at her. (4)
correction: pleased at her → pleased with her

q: He tried (1) / to open the cane (2) / by a cane opener. (3)
correction: to open the cane → to open the can

q: When the students reached late (1) / the teacher objected their entering the class (2) / without his permission. (3)
options: the teacher objected their entering the class

q: The captain and his (1) / wife were invited (2) / for the cultural (3) / function at my home. (4)
correction: for the cultural → to the cultural

q: The government decided to (1) / write of the debts (2) / of the farmers who were (3) / below the poverty line. (4)
correction: write of the debts → write off the debts

q: Children visiting the (1) / park are amused (2) / by the monkeys (3) / play in the cages. (4)
correction: play in the cages → playing in the cages

q: The great actor was (1) / angry with the treatment (2) / during the party (3) / in Ashoka Hall. (4)
correction: during the party → at the party

q: Don't go by his appearance, (1) / he is capable at (2) / doing any work (3) / without any hesitation. (4)
The teacher pointed out the (1) / students that they might (2) / learn something from Kalidas because (3) / Kalidas was the Shakespeare of India. (4)
options: Kalidas was the Shakespeare of India.

q: The consequence of (1) / the action was not fully realised (2) / to the authorities. (3)
options: the action was not fully realised
correction: to the authorities → by the authorities

q: I am pleased to sanction (1) / one special increment (2) / to all the employees (3) / with the month. (4)
correction: with the month → for the month

q: I have (1) / an appointment (2) / on the 9th of September (3) / on 5 o'clock. (4)
